mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lifts equity valuations by reducing discount rates and easing financial conditions, so declining policy rates should precede positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is falling for 3 consecutive months, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in retail sales indicates strengthening consumer demand and near-term revenue momentum, which should support S&P 500 gains over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if months with positive RSAFS growth are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal's 3-year rolling Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate signals a still-tight labor market and resilient household incomes, which supports earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if the combination of UNRATE below 4.0% and rising PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts reflects weakening rate-sensitive demand and a broader slowdown in cyclical activity, which should pressure equities lower over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive monthly declines in HOUST are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the signal loses significance in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates stronger output and utilization, which should improve cyclical earnings expectations and lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month trends in INDPRO fail to coincide with positive 60-day SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if the 3-year rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is rising while the policy rate remains low, real rates stay suppressed and liquidity conditions remain supportive, which should favor positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS below 2.0% does not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if its incremental predictive power over either series alone is negligible.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and rising recession risk, so a monthly jump in UNRATE should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE acceleration above 0.3pp over 3 months is not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the effect disappears after controlling for PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: A GDP growth slowdown combined with weak housing activity identifies broad final-demand deceleration, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is below its prior 4-quarter average and HOUST is declining
failure_condition: Reject if the joint slowdown condition does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of occurrences, or if the combined signal fails to outperform each proxy individually.
